title: delegation — cache keys + tags

keys.description: Cached result of DelegationResolver::activeAsDelegate($user, $tenant, $application). List of currently-effective delegations where the User is the delegate. THE hot-path cache — read on every RBAC permission check + every workflow/approvals approver resolution.
Reverse — list of active delegations where the User is the delegator. Powers /me/delegations + workflow/approvals selector substitution.
Powers the workflow/approvals selector engine. Given a role_id, list of active delegators (with their delegates) — the substitution set for approver resolution.
Fast lookup of an active impersonation session by session_token_hash. Read by the impersonation.enforce_active_session middleware on every request bearing an impersonation PAT.
Global gauge of currently-active impersonation sessions. Read by the active-impersonations-within-cap health probe + used by rate-limiting on the /impersonation/start route.
Guard for the one-at-a-time invariant — whether a specific PlatformUser already has an active session. Consulted by the observer's creating hook.

Compliance module's active-security-incident registry, cached for fast reads from the impersonation observer. When the flag is set, ALL new impersonation attempts against that tenant are refused with target_tenant_locked_down.

notes: TTLs are deliberately short (10-60s) because the state changes have real-time UX consequences (a newly-activated delegation should be honoured immediately in the next request; a terminated session must be refused immediately). The DB is the source of truth; caches are opportunistic speed-ups.

The `dlg:resolver:*` caches sit on the CRITICAL PATH of every authorization check + every approver resolution — cache miss rates matter for latency. Warm-cache on boot + short TTLs together keep the hit rate high without introducing staleness.
